memes.label: Adrian
memes.description: A Neapolitan lord and loyal companion of King Alonso, shipwrecked on Prospero's island with the royal party.

memes.label: Afric
memes.description: Afric (Africa), referenced in Act II as the location of Claribel's marriage to the King of Tunis.

memes.label: Alonso
memes.description: The King of Naples whose complicity in Antonio's usurpation drives the play's guilt-and-forgiveness arc

memes.label: Antonio
memes.description: Prospero's brother, whose usurpation of Milan set the play's events in motion and whose unrepentant silence in Act 5 is the play's most chilling moral void

memes.label: "Act I Scene 2 — Critical Apparatus"
memes.description: "Act I Scene 2 — Critical Apparatus — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Argier
memes.description: Algiers — Shakespeare's "Argier" — the North African city from which the witch Sycorax was banished, appearing only in Prospero's backstory narrative as the origin of the island's pre-colonial darkness

memes.label: Ariel
memes.description: The airy spirit bound to serve Prospero, whose powers of illusion, music, and elemental command drive the play's spectacular set-pieces, and whose longing for liberty gives emotional depth to his role as magical functionary

memes.label: Ariel's Imprisonment
memes.description: Sycorax's imprisonment of Ariel inside a cloven pine tree for twelve years, until Prospero's arrival on the island

memes.label: Art
memes.description: Prospero's learned magic—distinct from sorcery—as a form of knowledge derived from books and study

memes.label: Authority
memes.description: The right to command—political, magical, and moral—and the crisis of legitimacy that drives the conflicts in The Tempest

memes.label: Boatswain
memes.description: The sharp-tongued master of the ship in Act 1 whose defiance of royal authority during the storm establishes The Tempest's theme of disrupted hierarchy

memes.label: Caliban
memes.description: The original inhabitant of the island, enslaved by Prospero, whose contested humanity and lyrical sensibility make him The Tempest's most complex and irreducible figure

memes.label: Carthage
memes.description: Ancient city of Carthage, referenced in Act II's comic exchange about Dido and the geography of Tunis.

memes.label: Ceres
memes.description: The goddess of agriculture, presented as a spirit in Prospero's betrothal masque, who blesses Ferdinand and Miranda's union.

memes.label: Characters
memes.description: All characters in The Tempest, sorted alphabetically.

memes.label: Claribel
memes.description: Alonso's daughter, married to the King of Tunis — her wedding voyage sets the play's Mediterranean geography in motion.

memes.label: Concepts & Themes
memes.description: Key concepts, themes, and motifs in The Tempest.

memes.label: Court of Milan
memes.description: The ducal court of Milan under Prospero's rule, overthrown by Antonio's conspiracy with Alonso of Naples, and restored at the play's close when Prospero reclaims his dukedom

memes.label: Court of Naples
memes.description: The royal court of Alonso, King of Naples, whose complicity in Prospero's overthrow entangled the kingdom in guilt, and whose prince's marriage to Miranda heals the dynastic wound at the play's close

memes.label: "Critical Apparatus"
memes.description: "Critical Apparatus — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: "Dramatis Personæ"
memes.description: "Dramatis Personæ — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Dream
memes.description: The visions and altered states that dissolve the boundary between reality and illusion in The Tempest

memes.label: "Epilogue"
memes.description: "Epilogue — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Events
memes.description: Key events in The Tempest, in narrative order.

memes.label: Ferdinand
memes.description: The son of Alonso, heir to the throne of Naples, whose love for Miranda transforms the political enmity between Milan and Naples into dynastic union

memes.label: Forgiveness
memes.description: The moral resolution of The Tempest, enacted through Prospero's choice to forgive rather than revenge himself upon his enemies

memes.label: Fortune
memes.description: The providential force that guides the characters to the island and shapes the resolution of The Tempest

memes.label: Francisco
memes.description: A Neapolitan lord in Alonso's court, shipwrecked on Prospero's island alongside the king and his entourage.

memes.label: Freedom
memes.description: The condition sought by nearly every subordinated character in The Tempest, from Ariel to Caliban to the shipwrecked court

memes.label: Gonzalo
memes.description: The honest old counsellor of Naples who secretly provisioned Prospero's exile boat and whose vision of a utopian commonwealth embodies the play's most humane ideals

memes.label: Guilt
memes.description: The moral weight of past wrongs that haunts the characters and drives the movement toward reconciliation in The Tempest

memes.label: Harpy
memes.description: A mythical creature — part woman, part bird — whose form Ariel assumes in Act 3 to pronounce judgment upon Alonso and his court.

memes.label: Index
memes.description: Master index of all entries in The Tempest wiki, grouped by type.

memes.label: Iris
memes.description: A spirit in Prospero's betrothal masque, representing the goddess Iris, messenger of Juno and herald of the rainbow.

memes.label: Island
memes.description: The enchanted Mediterranean island that serves as the play's sole setting — simultaneously Prospero's prison and kingdom, Caliban's stolen inheritance, and a laboratory for competing claims to sovereignty, knowledge, and belonging

memes.label: John Fletcher
memes.description: Jacobean playwright (1579–1625), cited in the editorial notes to The Tempest as a parallel source — his collaboration with Francis Beaumont produced The Noble Gentleman.

memes.label: Juno
memes.description: Queen of the gods, presented as a spirit in Prospero's betrothal masque, who descends to bestow honour, riches, and marriage-blessing on the betrothed couple.

memes.label: King Leir (source play)
memes.description: An anonymous Elizabethan play, The True Chronicle History of King Leir (c. 1594), identified by editors as a probable source for Shakespeare's The Tempest.
aliases: The True Chronicle History of King Leir

memes.label: Knowledge
memes.description: The pursuit and possession of knowledge—particularly Prospero's secret studies and his library—as a source of power, vulnerability, and colonial domination in The Tempest

memes.label: Magic
memes.description: Prospero's supernatural art and its role as the central force driving the action of The Tempest

memes.label: Mariners
memes.description: The crew of the storm-tossed ship in Act 1, Scene 1, who labour to save the vessel before being charmed asleep by Ariel for the duration of the play.

memes.label: Masque (dramatic form)
memes.description: An elite Jacobean theatrical genre combining music, dance, spectacle, and allegory — the form Prospero appropriates for the betrothal pageant in Act 4.

memes.label: Master
memes.description: The Master of the storm-tossed ship in Act 1, Scene 1, who commands the vessel as it founders in the tempest conjured by Prospero.

memes.label: Milan
memes.description: The Italian city-state that Prospero ruled as duke before his usurpation by Antonio, the absent political centre that drives the play's backstory and provides its resolution — a place of learning that became a place of betrayal

memes.label: Miranda
memes.description: Prospero's fifteen-year-old daughter, raised in isolation on the island, whose innocence and capacity for wonder embody the play's regenerative impulse

memes.label: Music
memes.description: The recurring sonic motif in The Tempest that marks moments of wonder, transformation, and reconciliation

memes.label: Naples
memes.description: The kingdom ruled by Alonso, whose complicity in Prospero's overthrow drives the play's political stakes, and whose reconciliation with Prospero through the marriage of Ferdinand and Miranda heals the dynastic wound at the play's close

memes.label: Nature
memes.description: The contested boundary between innate disposition and learned behaviour, and between natural order and magical intervention

memes.label: "Notes"
memes.description: "Notes — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Nymph
memes.description: Nature spirits of classical mythology — sea-nymphs and Naiads — who populate Prospero's island and appear in the betrothal masque.

memes.label: Organizations & Courts
memes.description: Courts, factions, and groups in The Tempest.

memes.label: Places
memes.description: All locations in and referenced by The Tempest.

memes.label: Power
memes.description: The political, magical, and interpersonal power dynamics that structure every relationship in The Tempest, from Prospero's ducal authority and supernatural command to the micro-politics of the island's contested rule

memes.label: Prospero
memes.description: The rightful Duke of Milan, a scholar-magician whose twelve-year exile on an enchanted island culminates in the conjuring of a shipwreck that brings his enemies within reach, and whose journey from vengeance to forgiveness is the moral spine of the play

memes.label: Prospero's Cell
memes.description: Prospero's dwelling on the island — the "full poor cell" that serves as the play's central domestic space, the site of Miranda's education and Caliban's attempted violation, and the threshold before which key scenes unfold

memes.label: Prospero's Renunciation of Magic
memes.description: Prospero's climactic decision to renounce his magical powers, break his staff, and drown his book, restoring himself to ordinary humanity in Act 5

memes.label: Prospero's Usurpation
memes.description: Antonio's betrayal of his brother Prospero, the rightful Duke of Milan, conspiring with Alonso of Naples to depose him and seize the dukedom

memes.label: Sebastian
memes.description: Alonso's sarcastic brother who is seduced by Antonio into a murder conspiracy, becoming the play's second would-be usurper

memes.label: Servitude
memes.description: The master-servant relationships that structure the island's social order and raise questions about freedom, loyalty, and coercion

memes.label: Setebos
memes.description: The god of Sycorax, invoked by Caliban — a Patagonian deity whose name Shakespeare likely encountered in early modern travel narratives.

memes.label: Sleep
memes.description: The motif of sleep and waking that marks transitions of consciousness, power, and moral awareness throughout The Tempest

memes.label: "Sources"
memes.description: "Sources — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Stephano
memes.description: Alonso's drunken butler who, washed ashore with a bottle of wine, becomes the unwitting "king" of Caliban's absurd rebellion against Prospero

memes.label: Storm
memes.description: The literal and metaphorical tempest that opens the play and prefigures the emotional and political turmoil throughout

memes.label: Sycorax
memes.description: The Algerian witch who preceded Prospero on the island, mother of Caliban and former mistress of Ariel, whose malefic sorcery serves as the dark counterpoint to Prospero's learned art

memes.label: "The Tempest"
memes.description: "The Tempest — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: The Banquet
memes.description: Ariel, disguised as a Harpy, confronts Alonso and his court with a vanishing banquet and pronounces divine judgment for their sin against Prospero.

memes.label: The Betrothal
memes.description: Prospero's orchestrated betrothal of Ferdinand and Miranda, culminating in the masque and the stern warning about chastity, in Acts 3 and 4

memes.label: The Chess Game
memes.description: Prospero reveals Ferdinand and Miranda playing chess, reuniting Alonso with his son and sealing the Milan-Naples alliance through betrothal.

memes.label: The Conspiracy
memes.description: Caliban, Stephano, and Trinculo conspire to murder Prospero, only to be distracted by glittering garments and chased by spirit-hounds.

memes.label: The Masque
memes.description: The allegorical pageant of goddesses conjured by Prospero in Act 4 to bless the betrothal of Ferdinand and Miranda, shattered mid-performance by Prospero's recollection of Caliban's conspiracy — the play's most explicit meditation on the nature of theatre, art, and mortality

memes.label: The Mutiny
memes.description: Caliban, Stephano, and Trinculo's drunken plot to murder Prospero and seize the island, spanning Acts 2 through 4, culminating in farcical humiliation before the royal party in Act 5

memes.label: The Reconciliation
memes.description: The final act's cascade of revelations, reunions, and acts of forgiveness that restore the broken political and familial order

memes.label: The Ship
memes.description: The ship caught in the opening tempest of Act I, carrying Alonso, Ferdinand, and the court party from Tunis to Naples.

memes.label: The Shipwreck
memes.description: The spectacular storm conjured by Prospero through Ariel that opens the play, wrecks the King of Naples' ship, and strands the court on the island — a catastrophe that is revealed to be an elaborately staged illusion and the engine of the play's entire moral plot

memes.label: The Spirits
memes.description: The supernatural beings that inhabit Prospero's island, commanded by Ariel and conscripted into Prospero's magical service — the invisible labour force that makes the play's spectacular illusions possible

memes.label: "The Tempest"
memes.description: "The Tempest — William Shakespeare, The Tempest"

memes.label: Transformation
memes.description: The metamorphoses—physical, moral, and spiritual—that define the action and resolution of The Tempest

memes.label: Trinculo
memes.description: Alonso's jester, a cowardly and witty clown whose encounter with Caliban on the island becomes the catalyst for the drunken "rebellion" subplot

memes.label: Tunis
memes.description: The North African city where Alonso's daughter Claribel was married to the King of Tunis — a distant offstage location whose distance from Italy is itself a plot point in the play's geography of power

memes.label: Venus
memes.description: Roman goddess of love, explicitly banished from Prospero's betrothal masque in Act 4 — her absence marks the chaste character of the celebration.

memes.label: Widow Dido
memes.description: Queen of Carthage, invoked by the Neapolitan courtiers in Act 2 as a learned joke — a classical allusion woven through the play's Mediterranean geography.
